item.title: Mod Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
This discussion page is being locked as there's no need for it.

Please keep nonsense/no-context chatter to our IRC chat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I prefer the original tower defence ones. But that's just my opinion.

item.content:encoded: Sup

is "now mentioned"
since when has it not been

item.content:encoded: I've been on this site long before I had an account and I just figured out there was a rating and discussion for the front page of this wiki.

item.content:encoded: howdy ;)

item.title: Hi
item.content:encoded: Does this get used anymore? If so hello

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You need permission from the original author or staff. Never make major edits to articles without permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I want to add new content to articles, what should I do to get privileged?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Henlo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, information on this change is here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-10638442/top-bar-changes-march-2019

item.content:encoded: it’s pretty ok sometimes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a nice website

item.title: Re: Internet Outreach survey
item.content:encoded: Done! Thank you for bringing that to our attention!

item.title: Internet Outreach survey
item.content:encoded: The survey's closed now should the banner be removed?

item.title: Object classes
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this is spam. Please do not post comments like this, regardless of how silly the location is.

item.title: Re: Chrome is not function in the way Chrome was said to be function (but perhaps it is not the fault of Chrome)
item.content:encoded:
Strangely… yes. In all of them still (by all, I mean just Safari and Chrome, I deleted Firefox yesterday).

Perhaps it is the 4kon banner?

item.title: Re: Chrome is not function in the way Chrome was said to be function (but perhaps it is not the fault of Chrome)
item.content:encoded: …huh. I trimmed down one of the titles a bit more. Does the display still show up as weird for you?

item.title: Chrome is not function in the way Chrome was said to be function (but perhaps it is not the fault of Chrome)
item.content:encoded:
Based on what I saw, it was an issue with Firefox. Chrome was okay.

Uhh… a thing might be happening with my computer, because Firefox, Safari and Chrome are all showing the same box-overflowy-thingy this month. Maybe it's because of the 4kon banner?

For reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The July featured articles formatting is broken for me. Anyone else getting this?

Based on what I saw, it was an issue with Firefox. Chrome was okay.

e: I took a look at the page source, theres so many divs I can't make heads nor tails of whats wrong.

I've pared down the heading text for the top box, which seemed to be causing a weird overflow kinda thing? Hopefully should display okay now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The July featured articles formatting is broken for me. Anyone else getting this?

e: I took a look at the page source, theres so many divs I can't make heads nor tails of whats wrong.

e II: july, not june.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't know there was a discussion for the main page…

item.title: Glad it works on mobile now
item.content:encoded: Not sure how long ago it started working this well on mobile since I’ve just now checked back in on this community. Glad it does though.

item.title: Re: What happened to the logo?
item.content:encoded: June is LGBT Pride Month. The additional black and brown stripes represent the inclusion of people of color in the LGBTQ community. More info here and here.

item.title: What happened to the logo?
item.content:encoded: Why does it have an LGBT flag on it? And what's meaning of the black and brown on the flag? It's a paranormal/horror themed site, no? I haven't been here in quite some time so naturally I'm not going to be aware of every change and why.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: …not sure how you ended up here, but the discussion thread for the main page is not the place to announce new SCP articles. Please use the Announcements forum from the main listing: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum:start

item.title: New Scp! Scp-3507
item.content:encoded: A standard issue dumpster hiding a group of somethings sickly sinister…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, the 173rd comment is a rant about how bad wikidot is. How very fitting for this site of ours :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wikidot is throwing up errors and again and I can't write messages or post in the forums. I'm writing this here because I'm not submitting a bug error, I'm venting.

I wholeheartedly throw my (meaningless) support behind the nascent Project Foundation efforts. Wikidot is dying a slow, painful death, and frankly we don't have a choice. If we don't get our own website eventually this whole wikifarm is going to come crashing down with us still on it.

Just…wow, fuck Wikidot. We are the the sole reason they are a successful wikifarm and we have to deal with a crappily performing website and scam advertisements on a daily basis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added! In the future, for faster action, please send a wikidot PM to a staff member as well. Not everyone checks this discussion section very often.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: please, add Slang and Kuruni to SCP-TH.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had to downvote because it was at +175.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah! I actually hope that no one will upvote it at this point, or comment on it once the comments get past 173!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The comments are almost at 173, too!

item.title: I almost don't want to rate up this page.
item.content:encoded: As of Nov 7th, 2017 at 2:50 PM, the main page stands at +173 upvotes. Only insane people would upvote this page now!

item.title: Re: out of curiosity
item.content:encoded: This isn't quite the page to ask about that (you could have responded to that post in the discussion page) but yes, I did write a villanelle for 1468. It's in the collection here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/arbitrary-darkness

item.title: out of curiosity
item.content:encoded: did you happen to finish that Tale or other form of article on the chickadees? I noticed you said something about offering to do so in the Discussion page and I just had to ask.

item.title: Re: Oh
item.content:encoded: No worries. Also, I just handle the features, not the entire main page.

item.title: Oh
item.content:encoded:
I didn't realize that you specifically were in charge of the main page. I am sorry for nagging.

Note: as I realize that text does not allow body language and inflection and tone combined with the fact that I am a very brash and sarcastic person, I would like to point out that I am not being sarcastic currently. Please do not feel that I am speaking in a rude manner.

item.title: Re: Um
item.content:encoded:
Featured article has been updated.

this is what happens when zyn has fish-induced guilt seriously huxfish knew today was water change day and he built a bubble nest anyway so it got destroyed but now huxfish is all mopey and sulking on the bottom of the tank and maybe i should have just changed the water on another day when he didn't have a bubble nest in the tank and i'm just so worried about my fish hating me for wrecking his bubble nest

In all seriousness though, Sunday is usually chores day for Zyn, so I'm a bit slower with site stuff then.

item.title: Um
item.content:encoded:
My calendar reads June 11…so is the featured article supposed to be like that?

Edit: sorry, I should clarify that the EVENT feature is empty, not the SCP, Tale, or GoI, those are fine, but the event feature is empty.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Zachman1029, this is not the proper place to seek draft critique. You are supposed to use the Drafts and Critiques forum instead. Post it in this link instead: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-50864/help:drafts-and-critiques

This page is only the discussion page for the main page of the site. You will get no help for draft critique if you post such a request here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/zachman1029 can people read this and give me feed back it would be much appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You want the brainstorming and draft critique threads.
Preferably with some indication of what you want feedback on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can I get feedback?

item.title: New Sidebars
item.content:encoded:
I have checked out the new sidebars, and I must say that certainly is some sweet, sweet content they're now linking to. A New Posts thing is interesting (I didn't even know it existed), and a direct link to the Canons, GOIs, SCP Global, and Info Pages (as in, they're now their own dropdown) is great, among other things. But of course the best part is seeing those words 'Series IV' added, it marks a new age.

Who would've thought you could get so excited over sidebars?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the featured SCP has: Awarded fro winning the Alternate History Tale. Or something like that, and the winning tale has one for the SCP

That's correct. We don't put any limitations on features awarded as contest prizes, so winners can choose to feature whichever of their works they like.

In this case, the winner for the History contest tale category chose to feature one of their SCPs, and the winner of the SCP/GOI category chose to feature a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small Nitpick, but the featured SCP has: Awarded fro winning the Alternate History Tale. Or something like that, and the winning tale has one for the SCP

item.title: Re: A (very stupid) question
item.content:encoded: Thank you for reply, i appreciate it.

item.title: Re: A (very stupid) question
item.content:encoded:
For future reference, questions about the writing process should be asked in the Questions Desk forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk

For your question, the How to Write an SCP Guide, "Posting" tab has the information:

Go back to the Series page, scroll down, and click "Edit". This will allow you to change the series page. Replace [ACCESS DENIED] with the title of your article. This title is not in-universe, so DO NOT put this on your article itself. Save the Series page.

That said, it's highly recommended that you get your work reviewed by someone on the site before posting your new SCP article to a new series number.

item.title: A (very stupid) question
item.content:encoded: Excuse me for wasting your time with stupid questions, but how can i name my SCP? I find all those SCPs amazing, every unique SCP has a name, and they inspire me to make a new one, so i'd just like to ask… And again, i am so sorry for wasting your time. Thank you for reading this.
